policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding climate risk management guidelines requiring banks with assets over ₹50,000 crore to integrate physical and transition risk into PD/LGD models by March 2027. Banks must report climate-adjusted capital adequacy ratios quarterly starting April 2027.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Include Scope 3 Emissions for Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI mandated Scope 3 emissions disclosure under BRSR Core for the top 500 listed companies by market capitalization, effective FY2027-28. Third-party assurance requirements now extend to climate-related financial metrics.

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Framework for Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft regulations permitting parametric insurance products linked to weather indices, satellite data, and government weather station triggers for agricultural and MSME sectors. Public comments due by 15 May 2026.

policy.title: BCBS Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Standards
policy.description: Basel Committee published final Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks, mandating quantitative metrics for physical risk exposures by geography and sector. Implementation begins January 2028 for G-SIBs.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS updated climate scenarios to include sub-national physical risk projections for emerging markets, with specific hazard layers for South Asia covering flood, heat stress, and cyclone intensity through 2100.

policy.title: EU CSRD Reporting Requirements Take Effect for Large Non-EU Companies
policy.description: CSRD reporting obligations now apply to non-EU companies with EU revenues exceeding €150 million, requiring climate risk disclosures aligned with ESRS standards for FY2025 reports published in 2026.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for India Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter Intelligence announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide physical climate risk analytics for the bank's corporate and retail lending portfolios. The deployment covers flood, cyclone, and heat stress modeling across 15 Indian states.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $18M Series B to Expand Climate Risk Modules for Indian Banks
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed an $18 million Series B led by Lightspeed India, with plans to launch physical risk scoring for lending portfolios and BRSR compliance automation by Q3 2026.

competitors.title: MSCI Launches India-Specific Physical Climate Risk Dataset
competitors.description: MSCI ESG released granular physical risk scores for over 4,000 Indian corporate facilities, covering acute and chronic hazards at the asset level. The dataset integrates with MSCI's Climate Value-at-Risk model.

competitors.title: XDI Expands Cross Dependency Initiative to Cover Indian Infrastructure Assets
competitors.description: XDI launched physical risk analysis for Indian power, transport, and water infrastructure with cascading failure modeling. The product targets infrastructure lenders and development finance institutions.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Tata Consultancy Services for Enterprise Carbon Accounting
competitors.description: Watershed announced a partnership with TCS to offer integrated carbon accounting and decarbonization planning for large Indian enterprises, targeting compliance with SEBI BRSR and global CDP disclosures.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Launches Parametric Weather Derivative Platform for Agri-Lenders
competitors.description: CarbonMint released a parametric weather derivative platform enabling NBFCs and agri-lenders to hedge climate exposure on crop loan portfolios using IMD rainfall triggers.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: A Quantitative Assessment
research.description: RBI staff paper estimates that 12-18% of scheduled commercial bank exposures face high physical climate risk, with coastal and agricultural districts showing PD uplift of 45-80 basis points under RCP 4.5 by 2040.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Opportunities and Barriers
research.description: IFC report identifies a $4.2 billion addressable market for parametric insurance in India by 2030, citing basis risk and regulatory uncertainty as primary adoption barriers for financial institutions.

research.title: Climate Scenario Analysis for Banking Supervision: Lessons from Emerging Markets
research.description: BIS working paper recommends that emerging market supervisors adopt standardized physical risk stress tests, noting that data gaps in hazard exposure mapping remain a key implementation challenge.

research.title: ESG Data Quality in Emerging Markets: Coverage and Materiality Gaps
research.description: UNEP FI analysis finds that ESG data coverage for listed companies in India, Brazil, and South Africa averages 58%, compared to 89% in developed markets. Physical risk metrics show the widest gap at 34% coverage.

research.title: sigma 2/2026: Natural Catastrophes in 2025 — Insured Losses Reach $145 Billion
research.description: Swiss Re reports global insured nat-cat losses of $145 billion in 2025, with Asia-Pacific accounting for 28% of losses. India experienced $8.3 billion in economic losses from floods and cyclones, with only 12% insured.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate risk guidelines create a compliance-driven market for StepChange, but global players are moving fast into India.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: India's climate risk infrastructure for financial institutions is shifting from voluntary to mandatory, and both regulators and competitors are racing to define the market. The RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's Scope 3 expansion, and IRDAI's parametric framework collectively signal that Indian FIs face binding climate obligations within 12-18 months — exactly the window StepChange must capitalize on.

The RBI's April 8 guidelines are the most consequential policy development of the year. By requiring climate-adjusted PD/LGD models and quarterly capital reporting for banks above ₹50,000 crore, RBI has created a hard compliance deadline that banks cannot ignore. StepChange's core value proposition — translating physical hazard data into credit risk metrics — is now a regulatory necessity, not a nice-to-have. The RBI staff paper's finding that 12-18% of bank exposures face high physical risk gives us a credible anchor for sales conversations.

However, the competitor signals are sobering. Jupiter Intelligence's partnership with HDFC Bank and MSCI's India-specific physical risk dataset mean that global players are no longer treating India as a future market — they're deploying now. Sprih's $18M raise specifically targets climate risk modules for banks, positioning them as a direct domestic competitor. StepChange's advantage lies in deeper India hazard data and FI-specific model integration, but we must accelerate customer acquisition before global platforms establish incumbency with top-tier banks.

The IFC parametric insurance report validates our second strategic bet, identifying a $4.2 billion addressable market while highlighting basis risk and regulatory uncertainty as barriers. IRDAI's draft framework addresses the regulatory piece; StepChange should position our hazard data infrastructure as the solution to basis risk, enabling insurers and banks to design products with tighter index correlations. The Swiss Re sigma report's finding that only 12% of India's nat-cat losses are insured underscores the protection gap we can help close.

Watch for: The final IRDAI parametric regulations expected by June. Immediate action: Build a joint pitch for banks that connects RBI compliance (climate-adjusted PD/LGD) with parametric product design (IRDAI framework) — we can be the single data layer that powers both use cases.
